input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known breaking change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed in Android 11+ and MAUI has been evolving its approach. Let me provide guidance based on what I know.

In .NET MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach relied on Android's `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`, which was deprecated in Android API 30 (Android 11). Starting with .NET 9/.NET 10, MAUI moved toward edge-to-edge rendering and the Android `WindowInsetsCompat` API.


The issue is that `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` is no longer reliable in .NET 10, so the recommended approach now involves using `WindowInsetsCompat` listeners or handling keyboard insets directly within MAUI's edge-to-edge display model.

For .NET 10 MAUI on Android, the old `UseWindowSoftInputModeAdjust(WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize)` approach has limitations with newer Android versions, so I need to consider whether to use the manifest-level `adjustResize` setting, the newer `ImeInsets` padding approach, or keyboard inset listeners depending on the specific use case. Setting up the insets listener in MainActivity... Finishing the window insets handler and returning the consumed insets. Actually, I should step back—for a MAUI chat screen, there's probably a cleaner approach than diving into platform-specific window inset handling. Let me think through the practical options: I could set `adjustResize` in the manifest, use a Grid layout with the input at the bottom, or handle IME insets at the platform level if edge-to-edge rendering is causing issues. I'll focus on giving a straightforward solution that covers the main approaches.
